content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 24, 1994


William A. Romaine
Behrens, Snyder, & Romaine
1025 North Demaree Street
Visalia, CA 93291-4117


Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-94-261

Dear Mr. Romaine:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you on behalf of Judge Glenda Doan concerning the campaign
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Your letter accurately reflects the advice given to you.
Judge Glenda Doan is soliciting contributions for the purpose of
defending herself against charges brought by the Commission on
Judicial Performance which could potentially result in her removal
from office. Judge Doan was recently re-elected to office and the
Committee to Reelect Judge Glenda K. Doan (CRJGD), which was
formed to support that candidacy, has not be terminated.

The money she raises to defend herself against the charges
before the Commission on Judicial Performance are contributions
under the Act and must be deposited in the account of CRJGD and
reported by CRJGD on its campaign statement. It is not necessary
for CRJGD to change its name. If CRJGD uses "mass mailings" to
solicit contributions for Judge Doan's defense, its name and
address must be disclosed pursuant to the requirements of Section
84305 and Regulation 18435.

We are enclosing a copy of In re Montoya (1989) 12 FPPC Ops.
7, which discusses funds raised in connection with activities

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Romaine, William A.
No. A-94-261
.Page 2

related to an official's status as an officeholder and indicates
that funds received under such circumstances are contributions.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
[illegible]
By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

sections.conclusion: The money Judge Doan raises to defend herself against the charges before the Commission on Judicial Performance are contributions under the Act and must be deposited in the account of CRJGD and reported by CRJGD on its campaign statements. It is not necessary for CRJGD to change its name. If CRJGD uses "mass mailings" to solicit contributions for Judge Doan's defense, its name and address must be disclosed pursuant to the requirements of Section 84305 and Regulation 18435.
sections.question_synthetic: Must contributions raised by a recently re-elected judge to defend herself against Commission on Judicial Performance charges be deposited in her existing re-election campaign committee account and reported on campaign statements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Defense contributions are reportable contributions under the Political Reform Act that must be deposited in and reported through the existing Committee to Reelect Judge Glenda K. Doan account without requiring a name change. Mass mailings soliciting such contributions must include the committee's name and address disclosures.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice. No formal QUESTION section exists; the question was inferred from the letter's content. The CONCLUSION is drawn from the substantive advice paragraphs. Letter references Government Code sections and regulations for context.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Must contributions raised by a recently re-elected judge to defend herself against Commission on Judicial Performance charges be deposited in her existing re-election campaign committee account and reported on campaign statements?

CONCLUSION: The money Judge Doan raises to defend herself against the charges before the Commission on Judicial Performance are contributions under the Act and must be deposited in the account of CRJGD and reported by CRJGD on its campaign statements. It is not necessary for CRJGD to change its name. If CRJGD uses "mass mailings" to solicit contributions for Judge Doan's defense, its name and address must be disclosed pursuant to the requirements of Section 84305 and Regulation 18435.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice that campaign contributions raised by Judge Glenda Doan for her defense against Commission on Judicial Performance charges must be deposited in and reported through her existing re-election campaign committee account, with specific disclosure requirements for mass mailings.
